issuesFound.title: Missing heading skip
issuesFound.explanation: Heading hierarchy is not properly structured. The document has a single h1 ("Build accessible products — faster") followed by h2s, but no intermediate heading levels (h3, h4) to properly organize content sections.
issuesFound.fix:
Add h3 or h4 headings where appropriate to maintain proper heading flow. Example: Line 10: `

Build accessible products — faster

` → Add h3 for feature sections. Line 11: `

Everything you need to ship accessible software

` → Add h3 for feature cards. Line 12: `

How Luminary Studio works

` → Add h3 for steps. Line 13: `

Our tools

` → Add h3 for product categories. Line 14: `

Simple, transparent pricing

` → Add h3 for pricing plans. Line 15: `

Feature comparison

` → Add h3 for table rows. Line 16: `

What our customers say

` → Add h3 for testimonials. Line 17: `

Frequently asked questions

` → Add h3 for questions. Line 18: `

Stay up to date with accessibility

` → Add h3 for newsletter. Line 19: `

Get in touch

` → Add h3 for contact form. **Corrected structure:** ```html

Build accessible products — faster

Key Features

Call to Actions

Everything you need to ship accessible software

Automated Accessibility Scanner

Design System Checker

... ``` **Actual fix for this document:** Line 10: Add h3 for feature cards (e.g., `

Automated Accessibility Scanner

`) in features section. Line 11: Add h3 for steps in how-it-works section (e.g., `

Connect your project

`). Line 12: Add h3 for product categories in products section (e.g., `

Scanning

`). **Corrected code for specific sections:** Line 10: `

Automated Accessibility Scanner

` (add h3 before feature cards) Line 11: `

1

` (add h3 for step numbering) **Note:** Since the document has a single h1 followed by h2s, the actual skip is not present in the visible heading hierarchy. The issue is that there are no intermediate headings (h3, h4) to properly structure the content. The fix is to add h3 headings where appropriate. ---

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation:
Several form inputs lack accessible labels, making them inaccessible to screen readers. Users cannot understand what each field is for.
  Line 200: `<input id="search-input" type="search" name="q" placeholder="Search docs, blog, tools…" autocomplete="off" aria-label="Search Luminary Studio">` (has aria-label)

  Line 202: `<input id="contact-first-name" type="text" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required aria-required="true">` (missing label)
  Line 203: `<input id="contact-last-name" type="text" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name" required aria-required="true">` (missing label)

  Line 206: `<input id="contact-team-size" type="text" name="team_size">` (missing label, should be `<select>`)

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data inputs
issuesFound.explanation:
Several form inputs intended for personal data lack the `autocomplete` attribute, which is required for assistive technologies to correctly associate form fields with their labels and provide appropriate autocomplete functionality.
  Line 201: `<input id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="off">` (should include `autocomplete="email"`)
  Line 202: `<input id="contact-first-name" type="text" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name">` (correct)
  Line 203: `<input id="contact-last-name" type="text" name="last_name" autocomplete="family-name">` (correct)
  Line 204: `<input id="contact-email" type="email" name="email" autocomplete="work email">` (correct)
  Line 205: `<input id="contact-company" type="text" name="company" autocomplete="organization">` (correct)
  Line 206: `<select id="contact-team-size" name="team_size">` (missing autocomplete, should be `autocomplete="text"` or `autocomplete="none"`)
  Line 209: `<select id="contact-team-size" name="team_size">` (missing autocomplete, same issue)
  Line 210: `<textarea id="contact-message" name="message">` (not personal data, skip)

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation:
Several buttons control expandable content but lack the `aria-expanded` attribute, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Focus Management). Screen readers cannot announce the state of these buttons.
  Line 10: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu" aria-label="Open navigation menu" class="nav-toggle">` (has aria-expanded)
  Line 11: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu" aria-haspopup="true">Solutions</button>` (has aria-controls but no aria-expanded)
